attacking_style: Launches ultra-long sprints (200-500m) from distance, particularly effective on uphill finishes where pure sprinters fade. Opportunistically exploits breakaway hesitation in final 200m rather than relying on positioning.
team_tactics: Trek-Segafredo provides selective leadout support, but Pedersen often operates independently—disrupting rival trains, forcing stronger riders to lead out, or infiltrating breakaways for points classification gains rather than depending on pure train execution.
terrain_preference: Thrives on uphill sprint finishes and reduced bunch scenarios where diesel engine outlasts pure sprinters. Vulnerable on technical corners where positioning errors (trapped on barriers, overgeared) and premature launches cost results. Surprisingly resilient on hilly stages, recovering after being dropped.
vulnerability: Misjudges sustainable sprint distance in solo efforts (fades badly after 500m). Positioning errors in technical finales—launches too early, gets trapped on barriers, or crashes in echelon splits. Struggles when requiring pure acceleration from corners versus sustained power.
key_patterns: Wins uphill sprints by shadowing rivals' slipstreams then attacking final meters,beat Ackermann on 7% ramp (8 observations)
Infiltrates breakaways on mountain stages to hunt intermediate sprint points for green jersey tactical advantage (5 observations)
Recovers from being dropped on climbs to contest sprint finish, exceptional diesel engine capacity (4 observations)
Disrupts rival leadout trains rather than following wheel discipline, forces opponents into leadout role (3 observations)
Positioning errors in technical finales: trapped barriers, overgeared corners, premature launches in roundabout sprints (4 observations)
Form peaks during Grand Tours with consecutive stage wins once confidence builds from initial second places (6 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Grand Tour success completing stage win grand slam, maintaining form through mountain stages, though isolated 500m fade suggests power sustainability limits emerging
